name: wilson-pool
description: Route heavy Bash commands to a remote host roster via ssh (PreToolUse updatedInput). Each host carries a platform tag (macOS-only / Linux-only commands go to a matching host, else round-robined) and an optional per-host workdir override. workdir may be an explicit path or `auto` (mirror the current project — local ~/<rel> → remote ~/<rel>). `autosync on` rsyncs the local project to the target host before each routed command — a continuously-changing tree stays fresh with zero manual sync, and the workdir is created if absent (additive by default so remote build caches survive; `autosync mirror` adds rsync --delete). With `autosync off`, a pre-flight `test -d` check skips routing to any host that lacks the workdir (no more `cd: no such file`) — but a connection failure (ssh transport error / timeout) is never cached as `missing`, so a transient network blip skips routing for that one command only and never benches the host for the session; the host always stays in pool.json (removal is the explicit `rm`/`off` commands only). A PreToolUse guard denies a direct Write/Edit/MultiEdit to pool.json — the roster is sticky user intent, mutated only through the /wilson-pool:pool command (which writes the file via the plugin, not the Write tool), never edited by hand. A host may also carry a `sudo` tag: root-needing commands (apt, dpkg, yum, dnf, pacman, systemctl, ldconfig, setcap) are routable in their own right (they route to a Linux pool host even when the heavy `patterns` did not match) and are auto-prefixed with `sudo` on a sudo-tagged host — which asserts that host has passwordless sudo. External access (off-LAN: laptop on the road, mobile network) via tailscale: an optional `transport` knob (global pool.json key + optional per-host override; default `auto`) — `auto` uses `tailscale ssh <node>` when a local tailscale daemon is up else plain `ssh`, so the same roster works on the LAN and roaming with no SSH-key management; the routed command is `bash -lc`-wrapped so a non-login tailscale-ssh shell still gets the remote's login PATH. The `autosync on/mirror` rsync path stays on plain ssh (v1) — a tailscale-only host should run `autosync off`. CLI: `/wilson-pool:pool tailnet` lists tailnet nodes (name/OS/online/in-pool), `add <node> [tailscale|ssh]` auto-detects the platform from the tailnet and probes reachability, `status` adds a live per-host reachability probe + transport-in-use on top of `show`. Spirit-port of wilson's pool roster. Routing is OFF until the roster has >=1 host AND workdir is set; only the Bash tool is routed.
